items.title: Ontario now testing 10 people for hantavirus who have been self-isolating
items.summary_auto: Ontario's Ministry of Health says it is now testing 10 asymptomatic people with connections to a hantavirus-stricken cruise, a shift from government remarks made earlier this week.

items.title: Judge declares a mistrial in Harvey Weinstein's rape retrial
items.summary_auto: Harvey Weinstein's rape retrial in New York ended in a mistrial Friday after the jury deadlocked in the closely watched #MeToo-era case that another jury failed to decide last year.

items.title: 'It's going to be a Drake summer': Reactions pour in on Iceman's triple-album drop
items.summary_auto: Drake fans in Toronto and around the world were treated to not one but three "frozen" treats on Friday. The Toronto rapper delivered his highly anticipated album Iceman as promised at midnight — but then stunned fans by simultaneously dropping two additional albums, Habibti and Maid of Honour.

items.title: Senior in critical condition after alleged vehicle ramming in Vancouver's West End: police
items.summary_auto: The Vancouver Police Department (VPD) says a senior and two police officers are injured after a driver allegedly rammed several vehicles and drove along a sidewalk in the city's West End neighbourhood Friday morning.

items.title: Kendrick, Canada, extra albums and more: takeaways from Drake's Iceman
items.summary_auto: A breakdown of the standout moments from Drake's massive triple-album drop, from reigniting feuds to reuniting with allies.

items.title: Damage, debris left in wake of major spring Saskatchewan storm
items.summary_auto: A major weather event on Thursday in Saskatchewan included thunderstorms, dust storms and wind gusts reaching up to 115 km/h.

items.title: Ontario court dismisses Peter Nygard's appeal of sexual assault convictions
items.summary_auto: Ontario's top court has dismissed an appeal filed by former fashion mogul Peter Nygard, upholding his sexual assault convictions and prison sentence.

items.title: Supreme Court rules intimate partner violence can be basis for lawsuits
Italy caught a U.S. murder suspect on the run. Why did he slip past Canadian officials?
items.summary_auto: Lee Gilley, accused of murdering his pregnant wife nearly two years ago in Houston, ditched his ankle monitor on May 1 and was in Milan just over two days later. But the majority of the remaining questions concerning his flight from justice involve how he was able to board an Air Canada plane to get to Italy.

items.title: Ontario couple guilty of murdering boy, torturing brother in their care to be sentenced July 3
items.summary_auto: Sentencing for a couple found guilty of murdering a 12-year-old and confining and assaulting his younger brother while they were in their care over a number of years will be held July 3 in Milton, Ont. The Superior Court judge released his decisions in the Brandy Cooney-Becky Hamber case earlier this month.

items.title: Fierce winds, dust storm cause chaos in southern Manitoba
items.summary_auto: Hydro crews are dealing with dozens of power outages and people are picking up debris after severe winds on Thursday created havoc across southern Manitoba and reduced visibility in blowing dust.

items.title: B.C. gov't monitoring situation as yellow-legged hornet found at Washington state port
items.summary_auto: The hornets, native to Asia, are considered an invasive species in Canada and the U.S. and can cause serious problems for honey bee populations.

items.title: Hydro-Quebec fought to hide parts of 1960s records from N.L. government
items.summary_auto:
Hydro-Quebec fought to hide decades-old correspondence about a proposed aluminum smelter, saying its details
could derail ongoing energy negotiations with Newfoundland and Labrador.

items.title: Zwilling Kettles recalled by Health Canada due to potential burn risk
items.summary_auto: The kettle's handle can loosen or break off entirely during use, resulting in hot water spills and burns. Health Canada says affected consumers should stop using the kettles immediately and register for a refund.

items.title: 'An insult': Wood manufacturer unimpressed by $12M investment in B.C. forestry in face of U.S. tariffs
items.summary_auto: "It's peanuts compared to what is required,” says John Brink, whose workforce at a trio of wood manufacturing plants in and around Prince George, B.C., has dwindled from roughly 400 to about 30 people over the past year.

items.title: New Ebola outbreak confirmed in remote Congo province, with 65 deaths recorded
items.summary_auto: Africa CDC, the continent's top public health body, on Friday confirmed a new Ebola outbreak in Congo's remote Ituri province, with 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths recorded so far.

items.title: Zelenskyy vows retribution after Russian strike on Kyiv housing block kills 24
items.summary_auto: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy promised retribution against Russia on Friday after laying red roses at the rubble of a Kyiv apartment building where a Russian missile strike killed 24 people, including three children.

items.title: Halifax nurse marks fulfilment of lifelong dream
items.summary_auto: Tori Sabean spent years at the Halifax hospital after being diagnosed with leukemia as a child. Now she's working on the same floor where she was once a patient. The CBC's Carolyn Ray shares her story.

items.title: How much damage have Canada's booze bans done to the U.S. wine industry?
Military reprimanded soldiers who raised concerns about monitoring Canadians online during COVID-19
items.summary_auto: The military reprimanded soldiers after they raised concerns about an order to monitor Canadians' online activity during the COVID-19 pandemic, CBC News has learned.

items.title: More kids are running marathons. A teen's death reignites a debate about the risks
items.summary_auto: A 15-year-old girl collapsed and died near the finish line of the Leiden Marathon in the Netherlands on Sunday, sparking a conversation about the risks of long-distance running among teens and kids.

items.title: FIFA World Cup turns up gentrification pressures in Mexico City, say experts
items.summary_auto: Experts say parts of Mexico City have faced increasing pressure from gentrification which gained momentum during the pandemic — as digital nomads flocked to the city — and this has accelerated as the FIFA World Cup looms in June.

items.title: Will Canada embrace Dunkin'? Experts say the grip of Tim Hortons will be hard to shake
items.summary_auto: As U.S. café chain Dunkin' brews up a comeback north of the border, experts say they suspect the American brand will have a hard time luring Canadians away from the Tim Hortons menu they've come to know so well.

items.title: Why the U.S. is noticing this Canadian security bill
items.summary_auto: A Liberal government bill that proposes giving police and spies easier access to information during investigations has fallen into the crosshairs of U.S. tech giants and two American congressional committees, threatening to become the latest irritant in the Canada-U.S. relationship.

items.title: Canadian, U.S. fascist fight clubs joining forces south of the border, CBC investigation finds
items.summary_auto: Members of Canada's largest white nationalist group, Second Sons Canada, have met to train and network with a number of "active clubs" from the United States, and also met with active club movement founder Robert Rundo, who is currently on supervised release for violent offences.

items.title: Eurovision contestants set to compete in 2026 Grand Final — here's what to watch for
items.summary_auto: Eurovision — the global TV spectacle and music contest that helped launched ABBA and Céline Dion to superstardom — is expected to attract tens of millions of viewers for Saturday's Grand Final in Vienna, Austria, where 25 acts will compete. Expect polished pop, theatrical staging, a 50/50 jury‑and‑public vote likely to produce split results and at least one dominating performance.

items.title: Drake 'froze' the CN Tower in honour of his new album, Iceman
items.summary_auto: Drake wants to "freeze the world" with his new album, and he started by icing over the CN Tower Thurdsay. The Toronto rapper turned the city into his personal release party to hype his much-anticipated ninth album.

items.title: Trump leaves Beijing with few wins, but warm words for Xi
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump departed China on Friday touting business deals that gave markets little to cheer, while Beijing warned Washington about  mishandling  Taiwan and said its war with Iran should never have started.

items.title: Wet weather mixes with dust storm, brings rare dirty rain to southern Manitoba
items.summary_auto: If you were hoping for rain to wash away the post-winter grit and grime, you might be confused after the storm that just rolled through southern Manitoba.
